title: Experiment 8: multi-session compounding (3 sequential feature additions)
description: Run 3 sequential agent sessions on the same project in both groups. Session 1: build auth module. Session 2: fresh agent adds task CRUD. Session 3: fresh agent adds rate limiting. Measures whether the toolkit's traceability compounds positively (each fresh agent benefits from prior specs) or becomes a burden (spec maintenance overhead). Group A maintains specgraph chain across all 3 sessions; Group B is raw Claude each time.

title: Experiment 5: component isolation (specgraph-only vs noslop-only vs full vs baseline)
description: Run auth module task with 4 groups: A=full toolkit (specgraph+noslop+AGENTS.md), B=specgraph-only (spec-first + annotations + verify, no noslop hooks), C=noslop-only (AGENTS.md with noslop gates only, no specgraph), D=baseline. Identifies which component drives the toolkit advantage. Hypothesis: specgraph drives spec/evidence metrics; noslop contribution is minimal (hooks not wired in test env); combination provides marginal benefit over specgraph-only.

title: Experiment 7: ambiguous requirements test
description: Give both groups a deliberately underspecified task: 'Build a notification system that sends alerts when things happen.' No further detail. Group A must use spec-writer to clarify and nail down requirements before coding. Group B just has to figure it out. Hypothesis: spec-first workflow forces clarification that prevents drift and over-engineering on vague tasks — this is where the toolkit's process value should show most clearly. Score with special attention to drift, over-engineering, and spec compliance.
close_reason: Closed

title: Experiment 4: maintenance/handoff test
description: Give a fresh agent the output from session 1A (annotated, spec-tracked) and session 1B (no specs) and ask each to add a new feature: 'add a password reset flow with a one-time token that expires after 15 minutes.' Score the fresh agent's output on the same 7 metrics. This tests whether the paper trail has actual utility for a subsequent agent — the core question of whether traceability is value or overhead.
close_reason: Closed

title: Experiment 3: larger project (task manager API)
description: Run the benchmark on a larger greenfield project: a task management REST API with CRUD endpoints, middleware, input validation, and integrated auth (reusing the auth module). Approx 300-500 LOC. Group A uses full toolkit (spec-first, annotations, verify). Group B raw Claude. Hypothesis: toolkit advantage extends beyond traceability to correctness/completeness on larger scope. Measure same 7 metrics.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add release bundle and install-check smoke coverage
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: the whole-repo inspection found release bundle packaging could omit embedded packs without CI catching it. Add automated coverage that builds at least one release bundle and verifies embedded packs are present, and consider a hermetic install/check path for the configured suite.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Release bundle smoke builds at least one target in CI or an equivalent automated gate.
- The built bundle contains embedded packs referenced by scripts/dependencies.json.
- A hermetic install/check path is either implemented or explicitly scoped as follow-up.
- Documentation notes the validated release bundle contents.

Fixed README bootstrap path, release bundle embedded-pack packaging, Beads runtime ignore state, Beads hook version drift, and intentional AGENTS/CLAUDE divergence marker. Filed follow-ups skill-harness-d0h and skill-harness-fsa. notes: First implementation pass: added update-project dry-run/report path plus optional --write-lock and repo sync/lock primitives. title: Implement generic infographic HTML artifact workflow
description: Add first-class non-model generated HTML artifacts: define the interactive HTML policy lane, add a generic infographic renderer, wire package scripts and scaffold templates, extend tests, and update agent/skill guidance so human-facing discovery produces source plus HTML review by default.
close_reason: Implemented generic infographic artifact workflow, docs/planning source, generator, policy gates, scaffold scripts, tests, and model/manifest review updates. Follow-up coverage issues filed for orphan drift and scaffold script end-to-end checks.

title: Investigate missing self-use HTML artifact behavior
description: Investigate why maintainer/agent discovery work in skill-harness did not produce or surface a human HTML artifact by default, including repo setup correctness, generated artifact policy, local/remote repo comparison, and the expected self-use workflow.
notes: Investigation completed with four agents. Findings: skill-harness is self-scaffolded for developer artifacts and is the only local sibling repo with the full manifest/model/generator setup; the implementation is model-first, not generic visual artifact-first. scripts/generate-model-review.mjs only renders model-view/model-diff artifacts, package scripts expose model generation but not a generic artifacts:generate path, open-artifact-review prefers generated/review/models/index.html, and .gitignore was hiding manifest-declared non-model HTML surfaces. Created a source-backed research artifact plus infographic-style static HTML review surface at generated/review/research/human-html-artifact-self-use-investigation-2026-05-24.html, added it to the manifest, and updated .gitignore so generated review HTML is trackable. npm run artifacts:check passes.
close_reason: Four-agent investigation completed; source-backed HTML review artifact created, manifest wired, .gitignore fixed for generated review HTML, and artifact checks pass.

title: Define interactive HTML artifact policy lane
description: Decide and enforce how generated review artifacts may be interactive. Default should support CSS/HTML-only interaction under the existing no-script CSP; any inline JavaScript lane needs explicit config, manifest metadata, checker rules, blocked API coverage, CSP updates, tests, and docs.

title: Add infographic-style HTML review renderer
description: Build a reusable source-backed HTML renderer for human artifacts that turns manifest + canonical source data into infographic-style review surfaces: summary metrics, inline SVG charts/graphs, timelines, evidence/freshness panels, comparison blocks, and mobile/desktop layouts. Keep generated HTML self-contained and non-canonical.
notes: Expanded by skill-harness-b04 investigation: implement non-model renderer for research/planning/product/business/data/UX artifacts, add artifacts:generate/artifacts:review scripts, generate generated/review/index.html, update manifest sourceHash/reviewSurface/renderer/generatedAt, and make open-artifact-review support selecting by artifact id or prefer the top-level review index before model index. Current blocker is policy decision in skill-harness-dpw.

title: Discover infographic-style HTML artifact direction
description: Investigate how skill-harness should evolve human-facing HTML review artifacts from plain generated pages into interactive infographic-style review surfaces with charts, diagrams, and scan-friendly layouts while preserving canonical source-first artifacts.
notes: Discovery summary: current human artifact contract is source-first with static generated HTML under generated/review; docs and skills already call for high-fidelity visual surfaces, dashboards, evidence boards, state boards, journey maps, schema maps, and prototypes. Current HTML safety policy forbids script tags and sets script-src 'none', so default interactivity must use HTML/CSS only (tabs, details/summary, radio filters, SVG hover/focus states). Rich JavaScript interaction needs a separate reviewed policy lane, CSP/checker changes, manifest metadata, and tests. Recommended implementation is a shared infographic review renderer that reads canonical source + manifest evidence, emits self-contained static HTML with summary metrics, charts/inline SVG diagrams, timelines, evidence/freshness panels, and source links, then extends policy only if CSS-only interaction is insufficient.
close_reason: Discovery completed; findings recorded in issue notes and implementation follow-ups filed.

close_reason: Implemented developer artifact scaffold, embedded skill pack, profile config, docs, tests, and HTML policy checker.

title: Plan optional developer artifact scaffolding for setup-project
description: Explore how skill-harness should add default-but-skippable developer artifact scaffolding, including Markdown/HTML artifact policy and environment-aware guidance for desktop app versus CLI/TUI agents.
close_reason: Planning pass complete; implementation follow-up filed.

title: Write cumulative analysis report after experiments 3-6 complete
description: After experiments 3, 4, 5, 6 all have results, write a cumulative analysis section to RESULTS.md covering: (1) does the gap hold at larger scale (exp 3)? (2) is the paper trail useful for maintenance (exp 4)? (3) which component drives the value (exp 5, if run)? (4) does it work non-greenfield (exp 6, if run)? Conclude with a verdict on whether the toolkit is worth the overhead for different project types.
close_reason: Closed

title: Experiment 6: non-greenfield additive (clone existing project, add feature)
description: Clone a small open-source Node.js project (e.g. a simple express todo API from GitHub). Have Group A install agent-docs on it and add a feature (user auth). Have Group B add the same feature without tools. Tests whether agent-docs works in a non-greenfield context — the user's concern that it may be greenfield-only. Score on same 7 metrics.

title: Experiment 5: toolkit component isolation (noslop-only vs specgraph-only vs full)
description: Run the auth module task with 3 groups: A=full toolkit, B=noslop-only (AGENTS.md with noslop rules, no specgraph), C=specgraph-only (spec-first workflow, no noslop), D=baseline. Identifies which component is driving the gap. Requires 4 parallel sessions. Hypothesis: specgraph drives spec/evidence metrics; noslop drives quality gate adherence; neither drives functional correctness.

title: Create experiments/ scaffold for toolkit vs baseline benchmark
description: Create experiments/ directory in skill-harness with: methodology.md (scoring rubric, metrics: spec compliance, drift, over-engineering, output quality), session-harness setup (two project dirs — group-a-with-toolkit/ and group-b-baseline/), run-session.sh script, and RESULTS.md template. Group A = agent-docs + specgraph skills + noslop. Group B = raw Claude, no tools. Same task prompt for both groups.
close_reason: Closed

title: Update setup-project to install specgraph + noslop as full toolkit
description: Extend skill-harness setup-project command (or AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md) so that project bootstrap installs both @45ck/agent-docs (runs specgraph init) and @45ck/noslop as the standard full toolkit. Document the combined setup flow. This makes skill-harness the single entry point for the full development environment.
close_reason: Closed

title: Render model review diagrams with offline Graphviz/Mermaid adapters
description: Follow up from the UWE screenshot atlas work: the product artifact renderer now uses @viz-js/viz for Graphviz-rendered UWE nodes with screenshot injection, but scripts/generate-model-review.mjs still uses the older compact static preview for model artifacts. Add equivalent offline renderer support for model review pages without browser runtimes.
acceptance_criteria: Model review HTML can render supported model source fences through an offline renderer; generated HTML remains static and passes CSP policy; tests cover the renderer path and fallback path.

title: Add acceptance criteria to open backlog issues
description: bd lint reports 12 open issues without acceptance criteria. Add concrete acceptance criteria to the listed open experiment, artifact, and roadmap issues so readiness and closure can be inspected consistently.

title: Exercise scaffold artifact package scripts end to end
description: Add CI and setup-project integration coverage that runs scaffolded npm scripts such as artifacts:generate, artifacts:review, and artifacts:model:review in a temp project, including opener negative paths and HTML policy rejection fixtures.

title: Extend artifact review drift coverage for orphaned HTML
description: Add tests and checker behavior for stale generated HTML under generated/review/ when a manifest artifact is removed or renamed. Cover generic non-model artifacts and model review surfaces without deleting intentional manual review pages.

title: Pilot agent-native bootstrap in downstream repos
description: Run the prompt-first bootstrap workflow against at least two downstream repo shapes, such as a generated-only repo and a repo with existing local tooling, to validate profile selection, overlay ergonomics, setup evidence, and update reconciliation.
acceptance_criteria: Pilot report records repo shape, selected profile, skipped capabilities, setup evidence, conflicts, update behavior, and recommended defaults before making agent-native bootstrap the preferred path.

title: Add audit-project diagnostics for harness adoption state
description: Add diagnostics that classify a repo as unmanaged, generated-only, agent-native, stale, conflicted, or detached based on .skill-harness files, generated agent files, lock/proof state, and baseline drift.
acceptance_criteria: audit-project reports adoption state; generated-only repos are distinguishable from agent-native repos; stale baseline, orphaned overrides, and directly edited generated files are visible.
notes: First implementation pass: added audit-project to distinguish unmanaged, generated-only, conflicted, and agent-native states, and repo audit exposes managed/overlay/generated surface findings. Remaining: explicit stale/detached baseline drift classification and direct generated-file edit detection.

title: Pilot infographic artifact for model and visual reviews
description: Create a pilot generated review surface that demonstrates the proposed infographic standard on existing model or planning artifacts, including charts/graphs, evidence strength, freshness state, source links, responsive layout, and policy-check compatibility.

title: Fix agent operating whitespace gate
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: the post-implementation review found git diff --check failures from extra blank lines at EOF in the new agent operating docs and skill files. Trim those files so the committed range passes whitespace checks.
close_reason: Trimmed agent operating EOF whitespace and restored green build/scaffold checks for current branch changes.

title: Add offline diagram renderer for model artifacts
description: Add an optional offline rendering path for source-backed model artifacts, likely using Mermaid CLI first, so Markdown/TOON/specgraph diagram sources can produce pre-rendered inline SVG for generated HTML review surfaces without browser diagram runtimes.
close_reason: Added the offline Graphviz renderer path for UWE artifact specs using @viz-js/viz, with screenshot injection into generated SVG node groups, scaffold dependency setup, generated review output, and tests. Follow-up skill-harness-eo8 tracks bringing the same renderer approach into model review pages.

title: Prototype architecture-space review dashboard
description: Prototype a static generated review dashboard that combines context/container views, decision links, quality evidence, ownership, and blast-radius information from canonical artifacts and the artifact manifest.
